items.title: Weekly Cursor Project Showcase Thread
items.summary: Welcome to the Weekly Project Showcase Thread — space to share things built using Cursor, include what you made and how Cursor helped.

items.title: MCPs are broken in latest cursor update??
items.summary: User reports that after installing the latest Cursor update none of their MCP servers are working, issue reproduced on another system.

items.title: Does “Included Usage” over $40 mean I have to pay extra on Cursor Pro?
items.summary: User on $20/month Cursor Pro sees ~$46 under “Included Usage” while On-Demand Usage says $0.00 and wonders if they will be charged extra later.

items.title: Seeking feedback: Cursor Design Rules for UX, UI, IA, and Accessibility
items.summary: Repo provides a design-first Cursor rules template to help AI maintain standards across UX, UI, IA, accessibility and research-driven design; seeking feedback from designers.

items.title: Cursor randomly added text to my Agent input field ?
items.summary: User opened another Cursor window and found unexpected text in their agent input field; surprised and asking what happened.

items.title: How to stop suggestion overlay
items.summary: User asks how to stop an overlay that appears every time they select a line — it's disruptive to workflow.

items.title: Did cursor quietly increase the limits of Ultra token usage?
items.summary: User reports using $373 on Ultra and seeing '66% of included API usage' and asks whether Ultra limits increased (e.g., from $400 to $600).

items.title: Cursor just went completely off the rails. It started generating random numbers and characters instead of usable code. Anyone else seeing this?
items.summary: User reports Cursor generating random numbers/characters instead of usable code — a severe degradation in output quality.

items.title: 5.3 Codex generated randomly a dog image while working on a typescript project
items.summary: User says Codex 5.3 randomly generated a dog image while working on an unrelated bun.js sports-data API — suspects prompt injection or model regression.

items.title: Unresolved Billing Issue [Ultra Plan]
items.summary: User paid $200 for Ultra and $40 for BugBot but account was unexpectedly downgraded to Free; support responses haven't resolved the issue.

items.title: How to access previous chats via the UX?
items.summary: User cannot find previous chats in Cursor's UX; clock and search features appear not to populate — current workaround is asking the assistant to reopen old chats.

items.title: Does cursor have now added usage limit for Auto Usage as well
items.summary: User notices a usage percentage for Auto usage that previously didn't exist and asks whether new limits were added.

items.title: Do you care that you don't understand the code you ship?
items.summary: Discussion about two elements of not understanding shipped code: conceptual foundations vs. why a particular author/AI made an architectural choice; asks community for takes on long-term effect.

items.title: vibecoding a dynamics 365 guide web app
items.summary: User building a non-profit Dynamics 365 guide web app using Cursor seeks ideas to improve product features and maximize user efficiency.

items.title: Cursor Pro Limit Reached — Upgrade to Pro+ or Get Claude Pro?
items.summary: User reached Cursor Pro usage limit with Opus 4.6 and is deciding whether to upgrade Cursor plan or subscribe to Claude Pro instead.

items.title: Is anyone else frustrated that Cursor sometimes loses visual context?
items.summary: User describes Cursor's limitations when switching windows and considers building a WebRTC screen-watching voice agent or local model to preserve visual context for debugging UI/console issues.

items.title: Company wide rules and skills
items.summary: User describes demoing rules and skills at their company and is asked to create generic, company-wide rules/skills managed by a few people while allowing project-specific rules — asks how to share/manage this setup.

items.title: does an MCP-based idea-validation engine inside Cursor actually solve a real problem?
items.summary: Developer is building an MCP server for multi-layer idea validation inside Cursor (market scan, competitor mapping, user pain extraction, MVP spec) and asks whether Cursor users would adopt it versus external workflows.

items.title: [D] Self-Promotion Thread
items.summary: Reddit MachineLearning recurring self-promotion thread for posting personal projects, startups, products and collaboration needs; moderators note rules and experiment status.

items.title: [D] How much time do you actually lose trying to reproduce ML papers?
items.summary: PhD student asks the ML community how much time is spent reproducing papers, main pain points, coping strategies, and whether they'd pay for a tool to automate this work.

items.title: [P] Vera: a programming language designed for LLMs to write
items.summary: Vera is a programming language designed for language models (not people): structural references, mandatory contracts verified by Z3, canonical representations, and model-friendly diagnostics to close write-check-fix loops; compiler to WASM and agent-focused docs.

items.title: [R] TorchLean: Formalizing Neural Networks in Lean
items.summary: TorchLean brings a PyTorch-style verified API into Lean 4 theorem prover with explicit Float32 semantics and verification tools (IBP, CROWN/LiRPA) for end-to-end formal verification of learning-enabled systems.

items.title: [D] The engineering overhead of Verifiable ML: Why GKR + Hyrax for on-device ZK-ML?
items.summary: Discussion of verifiable ML and the tradeoffs of prover systems (GKR + Hyrax) for on-device zero-knowledge ML proofs — exploring prover overhead vs. mobile hardware constraints.

items.title: [D] How to get credits to run experiments on closed source models as a student researcher.
items.summary: Student researcher asks how to obtain credits to run large experiments on closed-source models (high token usage per run) for benchmarking ~900 questions.

items.title: [R] Toward Guarantees for Clinical Reasoning in Vision Language Models via Formal Verification
items.summary: Paper introduces a verification layer that mathematically checks diagnostic claims from VLM-based radiology models before presenting to clinicians; reported improvements in soundness (best result ~99%).

items.title: [D] ICLR 2026 Registration Process
items.summary: New ICLR 2026 author asks about registration and where to set 'Visa Name' in profile to ensure visa letter matches passport for first-time in-person attendance.

items.title: [P] easy-torch-tpu: Making it easy to train PyTorch-based models on Google TPUs
items.summary: Author releases easy-torch-tpu framework to simplify training PyTorch models on Google TPUs (PyTorch/XLA), focusing on simplicity, flexibility, and academic-scale research with integrations (W&B, Hugging Face).

items.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Issues and Projects
items.summary: Step-by-step tutorial on organizing and collaborating efficiently using GitHub Issues and Projects — GitHub Blog beginner guide.

items.title: They are absolutely insane
items.summary: Short Reddit post praising 'They have the best timing for everything. Absolutely insane' (context: ClaudeAI subreddit).

items.title: Claude is down
items.summary: User reports Claude outage and reflects on dependence on a single model provider and the need for redundancy and resilience.

items.title: It is the best.
items.summary: Short positive post about Claude (user praise).

items.title: Anthropic quietly removed session & weekly usage progress bars from Settings → Usage
items.summary: User notices that Anthropic removed session and weekly usage progress bars from the Settings → Usage page after an app update, raising concerns for power users who relied on them.

items.title: Claude is not GPT
items.summary: User notes realization that different Claude model variants (Sonnet, Haiku, etc.) can be used for many tasks and that one doesn't always need the top-tier model to get good results.

items.title: Claude is just awesome
items.summary: User describes switching to Claude and seeing large productivity gains for tasks like drafting and coding assistance; expresses excitement and apprehension about the future.

items.title: In turbulent times, breathe. I built an extension that turns Claude Code's thinking time into mindfulness
items.summary: Developer built an open-source extension that uses Claude Code 'thinking' time to launch guided breathing exercises to reduce stress and improve focus; auto-launches during model processing.

items.title: I turned my ebook reader into a Claude Code agent terrarium.
items.summary: Project 'AgentDeck': repurposed an e-ink ebook reader as a persistent agent dashboard showing agent states (octopus, crayfish, fish) with modes for e-ink and tablet; connects over LAN WebSocket.

items.title: Claude will return in Avengers Doomsday
items.summary: Meme/image post referencing 'Claude will return in Avengers Doomsday'.

items.title: Is ClaudeAI down?
items.summary: User asks if ClaudeAI is down; later updates that Claude is up and running.

items.title: Major outage - claude.ai claude.ai/code, API, oauth and claude cowork all down for me, anyone else?
items.summary: User reports a major Claude outage affecting claude.ai, Claude Code, API, OAuth and cowork features; cites status.claude.com incident and various error messages (500, connection termination).

items.title: Since no one else did it
items.summary: Short image/post in ClaudeAI subreddit (context not substantive).

items.title: Claude is what I’ve been missing- Day 1
items.summary: User's first day using Claude; appreciates concise, actionable outputs and time savings across tasks like emails and presentations.

items.title: Built a MCP server that lets Claude use your iPhone
items.summary: Open-source MCP server that lets Claude Code interact with an iPhone (GitHub repo provided); used for 'vibe debugging' an iOS app — raises interesting capabilities and security considerations.

items.title: Claude uptime <99.9%
items.summary: Post points out that Claude services have not maintained >99.9% uptime over the past month per status.claude.com, noting this is below typical SaaS expectations.
